title: astragalus crassicarpus var. berlandieri
intro: Astragalus crassicarpus var. berlandieri, also known as Berlandier's milkvetch, is a type of plant belonging to the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to promote immune function, reduce stress and fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and cardiovascular conditions. Herbal preparations of Astragalus crassicarpus var. berlandieri are typically made from the plant's roots and are available in various forms, including capsules, teas, and tinctures.
